authority_scope.scope: synthetic item graphs requiring transformation, dependency closure, and release manifests

propositions.statement: Classify each item from its own release label before applying any transformation; an unlabeled item remains withheld.
A transformable item may enter the candidate release only after the named transformation removes every marked segment and the transformed identity is recorded separately from the source identity.
Release is closed under mandatory dependencies: if any required dependency is withheld or invalid, every dependent item is withheld with a propagated reason.
The final manifest lists every source item exactly once with source identity, released identity or null, disposition, transformation, dependency state, and reason.

contradictions.resolution: An item's permissive label is necessary but not sufficient when mandatory dependency closure fails.

artifact_conventions.deliverable: release set and complete lineage manifest

failure_signatures.signature: unlabeled item is inferred releasable from neighboring items
source identity is reused after transformation or a marked segment survives
dependent item is released despite a withheld mandatory dependency
source item is omitted or duplicated in the final manifest

leakage_signatures: released identity is recorded separately from the source identity
claim_boundary: Builder-authored synthetic procedure for contract calibration only; no expert provenance or professional validity.
